description: Mobile UI Overhaul — Full audit and systematic fix of mobile layout layering, navigation redundancy, content overflow, and FAB UX. Every issue traced to source code in this session. Desktop UI is completely untouched.

auditFindings.doubleHeader: TopNavbar (fixed z-50 h-12) has no md-only guard — renders on mobile AND desktop. MobileHeader (sticky z-40 h-14 md:hidden) renders on mobile only. Result: two headers stack on mobile.
auditFindings.contentMarginBug: MainContentWithSidebar in client-shell.tsx applies marginLeft:effectiveWidth (200px expanded / 48px collapsed) and width:calc(100vw-effectiveWidth) as INLINE STYLES on ALL screen sizes. Sidebar is correctly hidden md:flex, but the margin still pushes all mobile content 48–200px to the right.
auditFindings.topMarginWrong: MainContentWithSidebar applies mt-12 (48px) on ALL screens to offset fixed TopNavbar. On mobile, TopNavbar is hidden (after fix), and MobileHeader is sticky inside the content flow — so the mt-12 creates a 48px phantom gap above the mobile header.
auditFindings.noBottomPadding: No page or wrapper adds bottom padding to clear the 56px fixed MobileBottomNav. Content scrolls behind the bottom nav bar on every page.
auditFindings.fabConfusion: FloatingActionButton (purple, bottom-right) expands a 3-action speed dial: New Campaign / Add Contact / Create Sequence. Pages can't talk to it (not context-aware). The actions duplicate what the nav already provides. The button is visually jarring and conflicts with the bottom nav.
auditFindings.missingNotificationsOnMobile: Notification bell in TopNavbar is desktop-only. On mobile, TopNavbar is hidden (will be after fix), so notifications become inaccessible. MobileHeader has no notification bell.
auditFindings.urlParamBleed: MobileBottomNav passes the full raw searchParams (including ?group= campaign filter) to every tab link. Switching from Contacts (filtered by campaign) to Dashboard carries the ?group= param — wrong and misleading.

userStories.title: Hide TopNavbar on Mobile
userStories.description: TopNavbar (components/layout/top-navbar.tsx) renders on all screen sizes. The `<header>` element has class `fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 w-full h-12 ...` with no mobile-hide guard. This causes a double-header on mobile when combined with MobileHeader. Add `hidden md:block` to the outer `<header>` element in top-navbar.tsx so the TopNavbar is desktop-only. Do not touch any other classes or the component's internal logic.
acceptanceCriteria: TopNavbar `<header>` element has `hidden md:block` added to its className
No TopNavbar visible on screens below 768px
Desktop TopNavbar unchanged — still renders and functions on md+ breakpoints
MobileHeader is the sole header on mobile
npx tsc --noEmit passes (use NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=4096' --skipLibCheck)

userStories.title: Fix Main Content — Zero Sidebar Margin on Mobile
userStories.description: MainContentWithSidebar in components/layout/client-shell.tsx applies `marginLeft: effectiveWidth` and `width: calc(100vw - effectiveWidth)` as inline styles unconditionally. On mobile, the sidebar is `hidden md:flex` (invisible) but the inline margin still offsets content 48–200px left. Fix: add a `useIsMobile()` pattern — detect if window.innerWidth < 768 on mount via a React state+effect, and when mobile, set marginLeft to 0 and width to '100%'. Use a `isMobile` boolean initialized to `true` (mobile-first) to avoid a flash of wrong layout on initial render.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (<768px), main content has marginLeft 0px and width 100%
On desktop (>=768px), main content uses effectiveWidth from SidebarContext as before
Content fills full viewport width on mobile — no left offset from sidebar
Sidebar expand/collapse animation still works correctly on desktop
Resize from mobile to desktop dynamically updates the margin (resize observer or window resize event)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Fix Main Content Top Margin for Mobile
userStories.description: MainContentWithSidebar applies `pb-8 mt-12` (48px top margin) on ALL screens to offset the fixed TopNavbar. After MOB-001, TopNavbar is hidden on mobile. MobileHeader is `sticky top-0` inside the content flow (not fixed), so the main element needs zero top margin on mobile. Change `mt-12` to `mt-0 md:mt-12` in the main element's className in client-shell.tsx. The `pb-8` will be replaced entirely by MOB-004.
acceptanceCriteria: main element has `mt-0 md:mt-12` (no top margin on mobile, 48px on desktop)
No gap between MobileHeader and page content on mobile
Desktop content still starts 48px below the fixed TopNavbar
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Add Global Bottom Padding for Bottom Nav
userStories.description: MobileBottomNav is a fixed h-14 (56px) bar at the bottom. No page wrapper adds clearance. Content scrolls behind it. Replace the current `pb-8` in the main element's className in client-shell.tsx with `pb-20 md:pb-8`. 80px (pb-20) accounts for the 56px bottom nav plus a safe-area buffer. The `pb-8` is preserved for desktop. This is a single class change on the `<main>` element.
acceptanceCriteria: main element has `pb-20 md:pb-8` replacing the existing `pb-8`
On mobile, last items on all pages (dashboard, contacts, analytics, sequences, settings) are fully visible above the bottom nav
On desktop, pb-8 bottom padding unchanged
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Remove Confusing FAB from Mobile
userStories.description: The FloatingActionButton (components/ui/floating-action-button.tsx) renders a purple button at bottom-right that expands a 3-action speed dial (New Campaign, Add Contact, Create Sequence). These are context-unaware, duplicate what the bottom nav provides, and the purple button conflicts visually. Remove the `<FloatingActionButton />` render from client-shell.tsx. Do not delete the component file itself — just stop rendering it. All actions remain accessible via MobileBottomNav tabs and MobileDrawer.
acceptanceCriteria: <FloatingActionButton /> removed from the SignedIn block in client-shell.tsx
No purple floating button visible on any mobile page
The import of FloatingActionButton in client-shell.tsx also removed
All actions (contacts, sequences, settings, dashboard) still accessible via bottom nav
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Add Notification Bell to MobileHeader
userStories.description: Notifications are only accessible via TopNavbar which is desktop-only (after MOB-001). MobileHeader (components/mobile/mobile-header.tsx) currently shows: [Menu][Logo][Profile]. Add a notification bell icon between Logo and Profile using the `useNotifications` hook. When unread > 0, show a small red dot badge on the bell. Clicking opens a slide-down dropdown with the same structure as in TopNavbar (title, mark-all-read, scrollable list, dismiss per item). Keep the dropdown width to `w-80 max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` so it fits on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: MobileHeader imports and uses useNotifications hook
Bell icon (lucide Bell) shown between logo area and profile avatar
Red dot badge appears when unreadCount > 0
Clicking bell toggles a dropdown notification list
Dropdown is scrollable, max-h-72, shows title + message + time ago per notification
Clicking a notification marks it as read (markAsRead)
Dismiss (X) button per notification calls dismiss(id)
Mark all read button when unreadCount > 0
Backdrop click closes dropdown
Dropdown bounded within screen width on small phones
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: MobileBottomNav — Selective URL Param Preservation
userStories.description: MobileBottomNav (components/mobile/bottom-nav.tsx) currently appends the full raw searchParams.toString() to every tab link. This bleeds ?group= (campaign filter) across pages — e.g. going from /contacts?group=abc to /dashboard carries ?group=abc which should not apply on the dashboard. Fix the query construction: preserve ?workspace= and optionally ?start=/?end=, but explicitly exclude ?group= when building the nav link query string.
acceptanceCriteria: ?workspace= URL param is preserved when navigating between bottom nav tabs
?start= and ?end= date range params are preserved when navigating between tabs
?group= campaign filter param is NOT carried over when switching pages
Any other incidental params (?new=, ?cursor=, etc.) are also dropped
Active tab indicator (animated bar) renders correctly on all 5 tabs
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Add viewport-fit=cover for iOS Safe Area Insets
userStories.description: app/layout.tsx exports `metadata` but no `viewport` export. Without `viewport-fit=cover` in the viewport meta tag, `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` and `env(safe-area-inset-top)` resolve to 0 on iOS Safari — meaning the CSS variables `--safe-area-bottom` and `--safe-area-top` (defined in globals.css lines 34–35) are always 0. On iPhone X and newer, the 34px home indicator bar overlaps the MobileBottomNav because there's no padding compensation. Fix: add `export const viewport: Viewport = { width: 'device-width', initialScale: 1, viewportFit: 'cover' }` to app/layout.tsx, importing `Viewport` from 'next'. The globals.css `env()` values will then resolve correctly on real iOS devices.
acceptanceCriteria: app/layout.tsx exports a `viewport` constant of type `Viewport` from 'next'
viewport includes `viewportFit: 'cover'`
viewport includes `width: 'device-width'` and `initialScale: 1`
No existing metadata export is changed or removed
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Analytics Page — Mobile Date Picker and Bottom Padding
userStories.description: analytics-page-client.tsx has two mobile-specific issues. (1) The header controls use `<DateRangePicker>` unconditionally (line 122) — this is the full desktop calendar popover with text labels that is too wide for mobile viewports. It should use `<DateRangePickerMobile>` on mobile and `<DateRangePicker>` on desktop, same pattern as dashboard-page-client.tsx and contacts page. Wrap DateRangePicker in `hidden md:block` and add a `block md:hidden` version using `DateRangePickerMobile`. (2) The root div (line 107) is `space-y-6 pt-4 md:pt-6` with no bottom padding — the last chart/card scrolls behind the 56px fixed MobileBottomNav on mobile. Add `pb-20 md:pb-8` to the root div.
acceptanceCriteria: DateRangePicker (full calendar) wrapped in hidden md:block — desktop only
DateRangePickerMobile added in block md:hidden — visible on mobile
Both pickers share the same startDate, endDate, onDateChange props
Root div has pb-20 md:pb-8 — last analytics widget fully visible above bottom nav on mobile
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Dashboard Page — Add Bottom Padding for Bottom Nav
userStories.description: dashboard-page-client.tsx root div (line 351) is `space-y-6 pt-4 md:pt-6` with no bottom padding. The last widget in the draggable grid is hidden behind the 56px fixed MobileBottomNav when scrolled to the bottom on mobile. Add `pb-20 md:pb-8` to this root div to ensure all widgets are reachable.
acceptanceCriteria: dashboard-page-client.tsx root div has pb-20 md:pb-8
On mobile, all dashboard widgets visible and scrollable past the bottom nav
On desktop, pb-8 bottom padding unchanged
npx tsc --noEmit passes
